concerns.text: SC-008 requires recording a specific marker string ('Dataset variable availability is confirmed...') if predictors are missing. T013 mentions generating this marker but conflates it with the Power Analysis task (T015) and does not explicitly mandate writing the marker to a log or artifact for verification, leaving the success criterion unverified.

Constitution Principle VII and plan.md require a fallback to LOFO if strata are small (<10 samples). T014 limits this fallback to 'training or test' strata having <10 samples, whereas the plan implies a general failure of stratified splitting if *any* family is underrepresented, potentially violating the generalization requirement.

FR-008 requires the *final report* to include a dedicated section stating findings are associational. T024 only tasks the generation of a 'reusable artifact' (disclaimer text) in the evaluation phase. There is no explicit task ensuring T031 (report generation) consumes this artifact to create the required dedicated section.

T030 requires the Cross-Family Transferability Test to depend on 'N-1 model training artifacts.' The plan's Phase 2 only defines a single Gradient Boosting model and a Linear baseline. The task implies a need for N-1 distinct model instances per family which are not explicitly defined or tracked in the plan's training phase, creating an implementation gap.

SC-005 defines a specific failure marker for missing columns ('DATA_MISSING: Required column [column_name] not found'). T012's description is slightly ambiguous about whether the *system* must generate this marker or if it just needs to handle the error; the task should explicitly mandate writing this exact string to a log or artifact to ensure the criterion is measurable.
